pressure_source: Vel's depleted Ledger and the urgency of reaching zero before she's too weak to survive the journey
state_shift: Vel commits to the journey; the countdown begins
must_land_beats: Vel's current level (31) demonstrated through the cost of healing
The Ledger mechanic shown through action — abilities cost permanent levels
Vel's decision to pursue zero now
Rumor of a Hoarded lord who walked out of the Vault
Vel checks Maren's route journal — battered, two handwritings, southern routes marked in detail

setups_to_plant.description: Vel's private urgency about reaching zero reads as unresolved grief for someone unnamed — a flicker of personal desperation beneath practical planning
Vel's assessment of what she still needs for the crossing includes a gap she cannot fill alone — reads as logistics, later revealed as the cascade mechanism's requirement for a high-level co-spender
Maren's route journal introduced as Vel's navigational tool — the handwriting isn't hers, the care she shows the pages reads as sentimental attachment to a practical object

pressure_source: Kem's revulsion at the Hoarded lifestyle and his father's visible deterioration into Stillness
state_shift: Kem breaks from the Hoarded; free, incompetent, and dropping fast
must_land_beats: The Conservatory Rite — the Hoarded philosophy of inaction performed as ritual
Lord Aldraste's Stillness: the cost of never acting, rendered physically
Kem's first ability use — absurdly overpowered for opening a door
Kem's immediate incompetence outside the Vault: Sustenance Charm that feeds forty
Kem takes the House signet from the ceremonial stand by the gate — not because he wants it, but because leaving it would mean he'd thought this through
Kem passes the sealed wing the Hoarded never discuss — a different quality of silence
secondary_character_beats: Lady Aldraste says Kem's name once as he leaves — maternal feeling compressed into a single word

setups_to_plant.description: Lord Aldraste's deterioration from level-300 preservation — the Hoarded philosophy has a physical cost they refuse to name
Brief reference in Kem's awareness of the Vault — a wing or quarter the Hoarded never discuss, where certain people are permitted to spend
Kem takes his House signet — a physical marker of Hoarded identity carried impulsively rather than deliberately, tracking his changing relationship to his origin

pressure_source: Mutual need and mutual disgust between two people who represent opposite failures of the Ledger system
state_shift: Uneasy alliance formed; Vel has knowledge, Kem has levels, neither trusts the other
must_land_beats: Vel's disgust at Kem's wastefulness — 286 after mere days
The terms: she guides, he follows, she controls information flow
What Vel conceals: the mechanism's specific requirement for a high-level spender

setups_to_plant.description: Vel's careful assessment of Kem's remaining levels and her private satisfaction that he solves her problem — reads as pragmatic alliance-building, later revealed as calculated manipulation

pressure_source: Hoarded outriders on their trail; Kem's inability to calibrate his own enormous power
state_shift: Kem discovers the joy and cost of action; pursuit confirmed; Vel realizes his recklessness is uncontrollable
must_land_beats: Kem's first real combat — power without technique
Vel's surgical efficiency vs. Kem's chaotic waste: 3 levels vs. 41 for comparable results
Kem's exhilaration — he has never felt his body work before
The pursuit is real: the Hoarded are sending people

pressure_source: The intimacy of shared vulnerability; Vel's resistance to connection
state_shift: Real trust begins; both characters become people to each other rather than opposing symbols
must_land_beats: Kem learning physical competence without abilities — cooking, fire, walking distance
Vel's origin story: the drowning girl, the Hoarded walking past, her first level gone
Kem's truth about his father — not the politics, the reality
A meal that costs nothing and means something

setups_to_plant.description: Vel's story of her first spend at seventeen — saving a drowning girl the Hoarded ignored — establishes her pattern of spending for others, which her later use of Kem will complicate

pressure_source: Institutional absurdity meeting real-world demands; Sable's first encounter with action
state_shift: The pursuit is organized; Sable begins her transformation; the Red Reserve is seeded as latent threat
must_land_beats: Sable's bureaucratic competence and field incompetence
The team of untrained level-300 agents — institutional absurdity at peak
Sable's first ability use and its complex emotional aftermath
The Red Reserve named and rejected as disproportionate — for now
secondary_character_beats: Lady Aldraste's political pressure felt through Council framing — retrieving Kem is institutional necessity, not parental rescue

setups_to_plant.description: The Council refuses to deploy the Red Reserve for a retrieval, framing it as an admission that inaction philosophy has failed — establishes trained spenders as an escalation the Hoarded are reluctant to authorize

payoffs_to_land.description: The Red Reserve is explicitly named and its nature clarified — a secret unit of Hoarded agents trained and permitted to spend for security purposes

pressure_source: Kem's privilege confronting lived reality; Dren's economic risk calculation against his family's needs
state_shift: The world expands beyond the Hoarded/Spent binary; Dren introduces the human cost of level economics
must_land_beats: The Spent Towns as functional communities the Hoarded dismiss
Dren's introduction: practical, profane, unimpressed by birth
Kem seeing working people for the first time — people who choose to spend
Dren's daughter Mira and the economic cost of her illness
Kem's House signet draws looks in the Spent Towns — a tavern keeper sees it and her face closes; his first shame at the symbol
secondary_character_beats: Dren's agreement is explicitly transactional — the payment might cover Mira's healing, and this calculation is visible in his negotiation

payoffs_to_land.description: The signet's social meaning outside the Vault — in the Spent Towns, the Hoarded crest is not respected but resented

chapter_engine: action / comic escalation shifting to genuine threat
pressure_source: Five untrained level-300 agents — raw power is both absurd and devastating when wielded without skill
state_shift: Vel forced below budget; Dren wounded and invested; Sable learns by watching; incompetence at 300 is dangerous and getting less incompetent
must_land_beats: Comedy of untrained agents causing accidental destruction
The shift from funny to dangerous: raw power hurts regardless of skill
Vel forced to overspend — cracked ribs from a glancing hit
Dren's silo-shrapnel wound as collateral damage
Sable watching and learning rather than fighting
secondary_character_beats: Dren's injury is not heroic — he is hit by debris from an explosion nobody aimed, which is how civilians get hurt in ability crossfire

pressure_source: The weight of diminishing numbers; the intimacy of care given by someone who has never cared for anyone
state_shift: Kem develops non-ability competence; the sub-Ledger's existence is revealed; the alliance deepens through shared vulnerability
must_land_beats: Kem performing field medicine badly but sincerely — ugly stitches, shaking hands
Kem at 178: the arithmetic of depletion becoming personal
Vel's partial disclosure: zero is a threshold, not a death; something exists beyond it
The sub-Ledger as concept — a world without the Ledger

pressure_source: Twelve years of silence between Vel and Thessa; the Hoarded's reach into personal relationships; Thessa's revelation planting the manipulation seed
state_shift: Vel's past catches up; Stillness encountered on-stage outside the Vault; the seed of Vel's manipulation planted through Thessa's revelation; Kem's trust develops its first hairline crack
must_land_beats: Vel and Thessa's history: partners who split over the question of spending
Thessa's Hoarded deal — economic security for route information
The Hoarded representative visibly affected by early-stage Stillness: speech slowing mid-sentence, eyes going intermittently vacant — Stillness rendered in present tense outside the Vault
Thessa reveals Vel came through years ago asking about high-level defectors — she has been looking for a Kem for a long time
Vel consults Maren's route journal to cross-reference Thessa's partial information; Thessa recognizes it
Kem burns 18 levels on defensive abilities during the escape from the Hoarded representative
The unspoken confrontation: Vel chose spending over people who loved her
secondary_character_beats: Thessa's fear is rendered as rational self-preservation — she has seen what spending does and chose survival, which is not the same as cowardice
The Hoarded representative's Stillness makes Thessa's deal morally complex: she's bargaining with someone the system is already consuming

setups_to_plant.description: Thessa's revelation that Vel has been searching for a high-level spender for years — Kem is not the first person she considered. Reads as troubling but not yet catastrophic. The seed grows through Ch 11-12 into the Ch 13 fracture.

payoffs_to_land.description: Maren's route journal is used for navigation and recognized by Thessa — the object gains emotional weight as a link to Vel's past
Stillness encountered in present tense outside the Vault — the Hoarded representative's deterioration gives the reader direct, non-backstory evidence of the system's cost

pressure_source: The need to solve physical problems without abilities; the danger of restraint becoming dogma; Sable's evolving question about spending
state_shift: Kem learns that restraint without judgment is just another form of ideology; Sable's institutional loyalty cracks alongside a question about what spending means
must_land_beats: Kem forced to cross the river without abilities — two hours of physical struggle, genuinely dangerous
The lesson differentiated from Ch 5: not 'experience matters more than stats' but 'refusing to spend can be its own form of hoarding — restraint without judgment is ideology'
Vel confronts Kem about bleeding levels on incidentals — 18 levels on Flame Charms, Strength boosts, and Light abilities since the Spent Towns
Sable's report to Lady Aldraste — what she says vs. what she withholds; the developing question: does spending make you more rather than less?
secondary_character_beats: Lady Aldraste's instructions to Sable are cold and institutional, but a pause or word choice betrays something personal
Sable's transformation expressed through growing competence at non-ability tasks and a question she can't name

pressure_source: The visible human cost of Hoarded exploitation rendered through bureaucratic absurdity; Kem's restraint lesson colliding with moral necessity
state_shift: The moral weight of the Ledger system becomes concrete through dark comedy; the Scour identified; Kem's 98-level spending spree dramatizes the cost of conscience; Dren makes his first non-transactional choice
must_land_beats: The Margins as exploited territory — indentured labor, debt-spending, depleted communities — rendered through bureaucratic dark comedy: Productivity Tiers, 'Renewable Expenditure Units,' interest rates on ability-debt
Fallow's unsentimental, darkly funny testimony: 'They called it a Development Opportunity. Thirty years of development. Very developed.'
The Scour identified as the threshold's geographic location

Kem's major spending: healing a sick child (8 levels), breaking an enforcement mechanism (20 levels), combat with enforcers (35 levels), helping repair infrastructure (15 levels), smaller interventions (20 levels) — 148 to 50, every spend dramatized as a moral choice
Vel watching Kem burn through her cascade budget and unable to tell him to stop, because stopping is what the Hoarded do
Maren's route journal consulted for the path to the Scour — Maren came through this region
secondary_character_beats: Dren's silence speaks — a man at 130 looking at people at 4 and doing subtraction on his own life; he decides to continue for non-transactional reasons after watching Kem spend for strangers
Fallow's account is matter-of-fact: they have seen people vanish and it is another thing that happens to people nobody protects

setups_to_plant.description: The Margins' moral reality — involuntary depletion, systemic exploitation — becomes a pressure that contaminates subsequent confrontations. Vel's heroic-spending narrative and Kem's liberation-through-burning narrative are both complicated.

pressure_source: The discovery that Vel engineered the alliance around the cascade mechanism; Kem's betrayal contaminated by the Margins' moral weight
state_shift: The cascade mechanism revealed; the alliance cracks over Vel's manipulation, now understood through the Margins' lens as systemic rather than just personal; Kem passes through Vel's starting level
must_land_beats: Kem's emergency road-collapse spend (12 levels) and incidentals (7 levels) dropping him from 50 to 31 — Vel's starting number
The cascade mechanism explained: co-spending in proximity stabilizes the threshold; high-to-zero descent amplifies the effect
Vel's manipulation exposed: she needed a high-level spender willing to burn down; Thessa's Ch 10 seed pays off — she's been looking for years
Kem's cold confrontation enhanced with Margins awareness: 'You recruited me as a component. That's what they do here. You replicated the system you claim to oppose.'
Vel's inability to answer because the accusation is accurate — the delay is its own answer
The alliance fractured but intact — they cannot survive alone at these levels
Kem's House signet: taken off his hand, considered for discarding, returned to his pocket

payoffs_to_land.description: Vel's strategic use of Kem is fully exposed — her level-assessment in Ch 3, her spending guidance, her concealment of requirements were all calculated
Vel's private planning about the crossing is revealed to have always included a high-level co-spender as a necessary component
Thessa's Ch 10 revelation pays off: Vel has been searching for a high-level spender for years, making the manipulation premeditated, not opportunistic
The Margins' moral reality contaminates Kem's confrontation — his accusation that Vel replicated the Hoarded's logic of using people gains force from the exploited landscape around them

pressure_source: The fractured alliance forced into mechanical cooperation; the cost of embodiment at near-zero levels
state_shift: Kem chooses to continue for non-transactional reasons; mechanical competence demonstrated despite emotional fracture; Kem briefly comprehends the Hoarded impulse toward preservation and rejects it
must_land_beats: Second consecutive Kem chapter — POV alternation broken; Vel's inner world goes silent
Communication collapsed to logistics; mechanical cooperation that works despite broken trust
Margins settlement rescue: collapsed mining substrate, family trapped — Vel directs, Kem executes with 6 levels of precise spending (31 to 25)
Dark comedy of the rescue: precision teamwork performed in emotional silence; bystander asks if they're partners, neither answers

Kem's flash of comprehension: exhausted at 25, he briefly understands why someone would choose never to feel any of this — the terror of full embodiment, the Hoarded impulse toward preservation. Not agreement. Comprehension. Then he looks at the rescued family and it passes.
Kem's decision to continue: not survival math, not new information — he started spending for other people and that person doesn't stop at 25
Kem's signet: a Margins child asks about it; Kem explains and is ashamed
Vel consults Maren's route journal; Kem notices the handwriting isn't hers and the care she shows it

setups_to_plant.description: Kem's brief understanding of the Hoarded impulse — the terror of embodiment — is a moment of complexity that complicates the novel's thesis without endorsing preservation. Filed for potential Book 2 resonance.

pressure_source: Sable's identity crisis meeting the fractured alliance; the revelation of the Hoarded's active threshold suppression; present-tense Stillness through the Margins defector
state_shift: Sable becomes a person rather than a force; the Hoarded revealed as active suppressors of the threshold; Sable's personal question drives her south; the Red Reserve threat activates with the permanent Scour posting
must_land_beats: Sable alone at 238, having dismissed her team; six weeks of transformation
Sable's encounter with a former Hoarded defector carrying Stillness damage — speech slowing, eyes drifting, the condition portable and visible outside the Vault
Three-way confrontation in a Margins farmhouse — Sable arrives to a broken alliance; dark comedy of bureaucratic speech patterns meeting raw reality
Kem tells Sable about Lord Aldraste's Stillness; Sable recognizes it from the defector she just met
Sable's choice: she cannot return him to what she now understands as slow death
Red Reserve has a permanent Scour posting — classified assignment, agents stationed for years — the Hoarded have been guarding the threshold longer than Vel realized
Sable heads south, not back to the Vault — driven by a personal question: if zero is a door, then everything she served was protecting people from freedom
secondary_character_beats: Sable's decision is a personal identity crisis, not a heroic defection — she is not joining anyone, she is failing in a way she cannot make herself regret
The Margins defector with Stillness damage makes the condition visceral and present-tense — not backstory but evidence

payoffs_to_land.description: Stillness encountered through both Kem's testimony and the Margins defector — Sable recognizes it from Council members she's worked with for decades, and the double confirmation breaks her institutional loyalty
The Red Reserve deployment is both new (Council authorization for Kem retrieval) and old (permanent Scour garrison) — the escalation reframes the Hoarded as active suppressors

setups_to_plant.description: Sable's personal question about zero — if the threshold is real, the entire Hoarded philosophy was suppressing freedom — drives her south and toward the Scour, seeding her essential contribution in Ch 18
The Red Reserve's permanent Scour posting reveals the Hoarded have been suppressing threshold knowledge for years — the garrison knows the terrain and the mechanism

pressure_source: Dren's departure exposing the journey from outside; Vel's grief and the demand for truth; the Margins' moral weight contaminating the confession
state_shift: Vel's motivation becomes fully personal and specific; the emotional core lands with Margins contamination; Kem chooses to continue through understanding, not forgiveness; Dren's departure is earned and felt
must_land_beats: Dren's full departure scene: his arm, his levels (120), his daughter waiting — what the journey looks like from outside Vel and Kem's urgency; 'My daughter is my zero'
Kem's ultimatum: no more steps without the truth
Vel's story of Maren: love, co-spending, the threshold opening, losing her

Vel's acknowledgment of moral asymmetry: pursuing a personal reunion through a landscape of involuntary depletion is a luxury the Margins' people cannot afford; she used Kem the way the system uses everyone
Vel's refusal to apologize for using Kem, but recognition that the accusation was accurate
Kem's question and Vel's answer: zero without Maren is still better than this
The repair is not forgiveness — Kem chooses to continue because Vel said the thing she couldn't before: recognition, not just information

payoffs_to_land.description: Vel's private grief, seeded as unnamed urgency in Ch 1 and obliquely through her origin story, is revealed as the loss of Maren — a specific person, a specific love, a specific moment at the threshold eight years ago

Vel's pattern of spending for others (established with the drowning-girl story) is complicated — she spent for Maren, she is spending Kem for Maren, and the distinction between selflessness and obsession is unresolved
The Margins' moral reality contaminates Vel's confession — her acknowledgment that her quest has the shape of privilege even without the intent

pressure_source: Near-zero levels vs. dangerous terrain and trained Red Reserve garrison; the late complication that the threshold has degraded
state_shift: Every remaining ability becomes life-or-death; the late complication reveals the threshold has degraded and two spenders may not be enough; the Hoarded's active suppression is confirmed; Sable's approach seeded through incremental decisions
must_land_beats: The Scour as landscape: vast erosion basin, exposed rock, hostile terrain
Red Reserve permanent garrison: a commander who has guarded the site for five years, witnessed three crossings, knows what zero is — plus two freshly deployed agents for the Kem retrieval; four total

The late complication: the commander reveals the threshold has degraded — natural erosion compounded by three years of deliberate dampening; a two-person cascade may not be enough; Vel's eight-year-old calculations are outdated
Vel at 16 and Kem at 18: the numbers against them rendered in dark comedy — 'I'm finally discreet enough for the Conservatory Rite'
The tunnel approach: Kem spends 6 levels (light, reinforcement, defensive), Vel spends 6 (Perception mapping, tunnel navigation, evasion); Vel at 8, Kem at 12
The commander as mirror-Sable: competent, spent, loyal to a flawed system, guarding something she knows is real because the alternative would collapse everything
Intercut: Sable trailing three hours behind at 236, spending on tracking, making incremental decisions toward the Scour

payoffs_to_land.description: The permanent Scour garrison is confirmed — the commander has been here for years, knows the threshold intimately, and has standing orders to prevent crossings

setups_to_plant.description: The threshold has been deliberately weakened — a two-person cascade may not generate enough energy. This creates a structural necessity for a third contributor, making Sable's arrival essential rather than convenient.

pressure_source: The final confrontation with almost nothing left; trained opponents with overwhelming power advantage; a degraded threshold that may not open
state_shift: Both protagonists cross the threshold; Sable's transformation completes in a structurally essential act; the commander's loss is the Hoarded system's first crack
must_land_beats: Red Reserve agents as precise, skilled opponents — four trained combatants including the commander who targets the cascade specifically
The fight at near-zero: experience and planning vs. trained power
Vel's planned ability combinations — maximum effect from minimum level cost
Kem's three critical spends: defensive burst saving Vel (5 levels, 12 to 7), precision disarm (2 levels, 7 to 5), push to threshold point (3 levels, 5 to 2)
The cascade mechanism activating as Vel reaches zero — threshold opens, flickering, degraded; the two-person cascade isn't enough
Sable's arrival: driven by the question, she burns 7 levels to stabilize the threshold — the third contributor the degraded site requires
Vel crossing the threshold — buildings, people, light without the Ledger's hum
Kem spending his last 2 levels and crossing
Sable remaining at 229, watching the threshold close

payoffs_to_land.description: The degraded threshold fails to sustain a two-person cascade — the complication from Ch 17 pays off as Sable's contribution becomes structurally essential
Sable's personal question about zero reaches its answer — she sees the door, confirms it's real, and makes her most consequential choice: stabilizing it for others to cross

pressure_source: The weight of arrival; the fragility of the sub-Ledger's secrecy now that the crossing has been witnessed; the political complexity of the world below
state_shift: The destination reached; the sub-Ledger rendered as a specific, politically complex civilization; personal quests resolve imperfectly; Sable protects the sub-Ledger with a lie; the saga question opens
must_land_beats: The sub-Ledger's silence: the Ledger's background hum is gone — the absence is physical, like a phantom limb in reverse

The sub-Ledger as a specific civilization: medicine with obsidian-edge tools and herbalism apprenticeships; governance by council with real tensions between voluntary and involuntary zeros; architecture of hand-quarried stone showing tool marks
Vel finding Maren: a reunion that is complex — eight years apart, Maren has built a life, neither is the person who left; but Vel is there and Maren is there
Kem in a world without the Ledger: just hands, no stats, something close to peace
Kem's House signet set on a windowsill — just a ring, meaning nothing down here
The threat: the Hoarded know about the threshold now — the Reserve witnessed the crossing

Intercut — Sable at 229 at the threshold's edge: she chooses to file a false report ('Level-zero termination. No threshold event observed.'), protecting the sub-Ledger with a lie that won't hold forever
Book 1 ending: everything to protect, nothing left to spend

payoffs_to_land.description: The House signet reaches its final position: set on a windowsill in the sub-Ledger, stripped of meaning — Kem's identity transformation rendered through an object's journey from ceremonial stand to pocket to windowsill

Kem's Ch 14 flash of comprehension — understanding why someone might choose preservation — resonates in the sub-Ledger: he chose embodiment, and now he lives with the full weight of that choice in a world that demands everything from the body
